fact: April 16 is exactly 106 days into the year - that's like counting to 106 every single morning since January 1st!
In the Northern Hemisphere, earthworms are going absolutely crazy right now, wiggling up to the surface after spring rains and making birds super happy for easy snacks.
Cherry blossom petals are typically falling like pink snow in many parts of the world around this time, creating natural confetti carpets on sidewalks and streets.
The sun is rising about 2 minutes earlier each day around this time in most places, which means your morning is getting brighter faster than you can notice!
Aries season is almost over - people born on April 16 are some of the last ram-powered zodiac babies before Taurus takes over on April 20th.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, leaves are turning brilliant orange and red right now as autumn kicks into high gear.
Migrating butterflies like painted ladies are often spotted in huge swarms around this date, sometimes covering entire hillsides in fluttering orange wings.
Dandelions are usually exploding into their fluffy white seed heads right about now, ready to blow wishes and spread weeds everywhere with just one puff of wind.
In northern regions, this is typically prime maple syrup season - tree sap is flowing like crazy as temperatures bounce between freezing nights and warm days.
April 16 sits almost perfectly in the middle of spring's 93-day run, making it like the 'teenage years' of the spring season.
Frogs in ponds and marshes are usually reaching peak croaking volume around this time, creating nature's loudest nighttime concerts as they search for mates.
